growth_records.medium: DSM 141c medium, bicarbonate-buffered, minus trimethylamine, supplemented with ferrihydrite as electron acceptor and methane headspace

up.units: mol per mol CH4 (normalization basis of balance Eq.2)
up.method: substrate oxidized; growth tracked as protein increase (Fig.2C)

up.units: mol Fe3+ reduced per mol CH4 oxidized
up.method: carbon/electron balance Eq.2; Fe(II) increase measured (ferrozine)

up.units: mol per mol CH4 (net consumed in balance)
up.method: carbon/electron balance Eq.2 (CO2 on reactant side)

sec.method: carbon/electron balance Eq.2; formate identified as product (Fig.4)

growth_records.cite: Yan et al. Respiration-driven methanotrophic growth of diverse marine methanogens. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2303179120. [PMC10523532]
growth_records.snippet: the Y_ATP (g dry weight/mole) is 15.7 for M. orientis sp. nov. ... The Y_DX (C-mol substrate/C-mol biomass) for M. orientis sp. nov. (0.78) ... Acetate was not detected during growth of M. orientis sp. nov.
growth_records.notes: Ferrihydrite-dependent methanotrophic growth. No doubling time / growth rate reported for M. orientis (29 d value is M. acetivorans-specific), so growth_rate/doubling_time left null. Stoichiometric fluxes per mol CH4 from balance Eq.2: CH4 + 0.03 CO2 + 0.17 NH4Cl + 4.12 Fe3+ + 0.91 H2O = 0.31 CH2O2(formate) + 4.12 Fe2+ + 4.12 H+ + 0.17 HCl + 0.72 CH1.77O0.49N0.24(biomass). No acetate produced (verbatim). Y_ATP=15.7 gDW/mol ATP, Y_DX=0.78 C-mol/C-mol verbatim. Greater biomass yield than M. acetivorans consistent with higher Y_ATP.

growth_records.medium: DSM 141c medium, bicarbonate-buffered, minus trimethylamine, supplemented with humic acids as electron acceptor and methane headspace

up.method: protein/ATP increase dependent on methane and electron acceptor (Fig.2)
humic acids more favorable than ferrihydrite for respiratory growth

sec.method: formate is end product for M. orientis (Fig.4); acetate not detected

growth_records.cite: Yan et al. Respiration-driven methanotrophic growth of diverse marine methanogens. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2303179120. [PMC10523532]
growth_records.snippet: Growth of M. orientis sp. nov. accompanied methane consumption ... Humic acids were more favorable than ferrihydrite for respiratory growth ... Formate was a product for M. orientis sp. nov. ... Acetate was not detected during growth of M.
growth_records.notes: Humic-acid-dependent methanotrophic growth of M. orientis. No numeric growth rate/doubling time reported for humic-acid condition. Formate is the only organic-acid product (no acetate). No quantitative flux derivable for this specific condition (balance Eq.2 was normalized to the ferrihydrite condition).
